definition: school (an institution dedicated to teaching and learning)
time (the moment, as indicated by a clock or similar device)
to look at (to try to see, to pay attention to with one’s eyes)
much, a lot, far, way, many times
night (the period between sunset and sunrise)
oh (expression of awe, surprise, pain or realization)
bread (food made by baking cereal dough)
dog (# The species Canis familiaris (sometimes designated Canis lupus familiaris), domesticated for thousands of years and of highly variable appearance because of human breeding.)
former (in contrast to the latter), first, first one
problem, issue, challenge (a difficulty or obstacle that has to be dealt with)
mister, sir, lord (title conferred on a married or older male)
to see, to look at, to view (perceive)
there: used to designate a place near the listener
before (earlier than) (triggers the subjunctive in subordinate clauses)
to seek, search for, look for, try to find, to scour
to think, to believe, to reckon, to be of the opinion
sorry? what? (expressing surprise or asking to repeat)
to go (away from speaker and listener)
to think of, think that (have an opinion on)
power (the ability to coerce, influence or control)
to desire, to want, to want to
to go out, to leave, to depart, to head out
Senses relating to literal movement. [+de (from) or desde (from), along with a (to) or para (to, toward) or hacia (toward)]
to know (a person or a place), to be familiar with, to be acquainted with
must, need to, have to (indicates an obligation or requirement) [(optionally) +de]
to wait, wait (for) [(optionally) +direct object (for something) or a que (+ subjunctive clause) (for something to happen)]
to be (denotes a copula, in a transient fashion)

translated as "to like", analyzable in structure as "to please" [+dative (someone)] (the thing being liked is the subject, while the "liker" is in the dative case ("indirect object")). See the usage notes for more. Compare similar structures in Italian "piacere", Romanian "plăcea", German "gefallen" and Russian "нравиться"
to take, to carry, to take away, to carry away, to carry around, to bring, to bear, to lug (implies to move something further from who speaks)
appearance (used generally when a person is pretty)
to put, to put up, to place, to lay
to try, to seek, to attempt, to strive, to endeavor [+de (+ infinitive) (to do something)]
dolled up, smartened up, dressed for a special occasion
